mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate signals a strong labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC less than 0.05 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods with UNRATE below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% per annum indicates restrictive monetary policy that slows economic activity and reduces corporate profits, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 40% of triggered periods despite FEDFUNDS > 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are high (above 700,000 million dollars) alongside high federal funds rates (above 5%), it signals an overheated economy where the Fed is likely to keep rates high, dampening future growth and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal exceeds 0 in 60% of out-of-sample periods, but forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime, industrial production rising above the 100 index level indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which boosts earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when INDPRO is above 100, the 60-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,000 thousand units reflect strong housing demand, which stimulates related industries and consumer spending, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1000, 20-day forward returns are negative in a majority of cases, or the average return is below 0.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of real GDP indicates a large economic base that supports corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 level and 60-day forward returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is high (above 158 million) and the federal funds rate is high (above 5%), it signals a fully employed economy with tight monetary policy, often preceding an economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In such triggered periods, 20-day forward returns are positive more than half the time, or the interaction coefficient is not consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 310 suggests elevated inflation pressure, which prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, reducing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 310, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the inflation-return correlation is not negative.
